metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend or replace the `test_docs_document_provenance` assertions that AC6's proof relies on so they actually check: (a) `docs/config.md` no longer contains the literal string `claude-code_<ver>`, and (b) `docs/config.md` contains the phrase `base name` alongside `claude-code` — otherwise the proof command green-lights a no-op.
findings.finding: AC6's proof command `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q` points at the existing `test_docs_document_provenance` function (lines 191-197), which only asserts `'AI_AGENT' in cfg` and `'OPENCODE_SESSION_ID' in cfg`. It does NOT assert the absence of the `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` slash-form, nor the presence of `base name claude-code` — meaning the proof command already passes today before any vocabulary edits, making it unable to verify the claimed change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 / tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py:191-197
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `test_docs_document_provenance` (or a new `-k docs` test): `assert 'claude-code_<ver>' not in cfg` and `assert 'base name' in cfg and 'claude-code' in cfg` (or match the exact new phrasing). Also add the equivalent negative+positive assertions against `docs/event-schema.md` and the `session_id.py` comment text.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` to the Scope/IN edit list and describe the specific changes needed (new assertions in `test_docs_document_provenance`, and whether `test_resolve_harness`'s `claude-code_1.2.3` fixture value remains valid under the new vocabulary).
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` is an unenumerated edit target: AC6 cites `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q` as the proof command for vocabulary-alignment, but the existing `test_docs_document_provenance` test only asserts `'AI_AGENT' in cfg` and `'OPENCODE_SESSION_ID' in cfg` — it does NOT contain any assertion for absence of the old slash-form nor presence of 'base name `claude-code`'. The test must therefore be modified to pass AC6, yet the file appears nowhere in the plan's Scope or What sections.
findings.location: Scope section (IN list) and AC6 proof command (`pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, change the IN list to include `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` and note: 'extend `test_docs_document_provenance` to assert `docs/config.md` no longer contains the slash-form example and does contain the base-name form'. Also confirm in the What/vocabulary-alignment bullet that `test_resolve_harness` using `claude-code_1.2.3` remains valid (versioned form is still accepted).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` as an edit target in Scope and specify the new assertions to add to `test_docs_document_provenance` (absence of the old slash-form string, presence of 'base name `claude-code`') so AC6's proof is non-trivial and executor-actionable.
findings.finding: AC6's cited proof command (`pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q`) points at the existing `test_docs_document_provenance` function (lines 191–197), which only asserts `'AI_AGENT' in cfg` and `'OPENCODE_SESSION_ID' in cfg` — it does NOT assert the absence of the old `claude-code_<ver>` slash-form nor the presence of the new 'base name `claude-code`' language. The plan says the test DOES assert these things, but the existing test body contradicts this. An executor reading the AC might accept the existing test as already passing AC6 without writing the required new assertions.
findings.location: AC6 (Acceptance Criteria, last bullet); tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py lines 191–197
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Update `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py::test_docs_document_provenance` to assert: (a) `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` slash-form is absent from `docs/config.md`; (b) the phrase `base name \`claude-code\`` is present in `docs/config.md`.' This makes the proof command non-trivially satisfiable.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC #2) to explicitly assert that when stdin contains a valid `session_id`, the script writes `AI_AGENT=claude-code` (no version suffix) — covering the 'version' test case named in Scope.
findings.finding: The Scope enumerates 'version' as a distinct test case ('valid shell + env-file output for mock stdin, version, malformed, and no-op cases'), but no AC explicitly covers a 'version' scenario — leaving it an orphan described deliverable with no verifiable criterion.
findings.location: Scope section (test case list) vs. Acceptance Criteria (AC #1–#5)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #2: 'the script writes `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=abc123` AND `export AI_AGENT=claude-code` (bare base name, no `_<version>` suffix) to that file. Proof: same pytest invocation, with an additional assertion that the AI_AGENT line does not match `claude-code_.*`.' Then drop the standalone 'version' label from Scope or relabel it 'bare-base-name'.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'version' test case in an AC: specify the stdin shape (e.g., does the SessionStart envelope include a `version` field?), the expected script behavior (ignore it? use it to populate `AI_AGENT=claude-code_<ver>`?), and what passing looks like — or remove 'version' from the Scope test-case list if it is redundant with another named case.
findings.finding: The Scope section names 'version' as a distinct test case for `test_session_shims.py` ('valid shell + env-file output for mock stdin, version, malformed, and no-op cases'), but no AC defines what the 'version' test case is — what stdin shape triggers it, what the expected output or behavior is, or what 'version' refers to in the SessionStart hook context.
findings.location: Scope section — 'a unit test validating the script (valid shell + env-file output for mock stdin, version, malformed, and no-op cases)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the complete updated canonical `AI_AGENT` / `claim_harness` vocabulary (e.g., `claude-code` as valid base name, `claude-code_<ver>` still valid with optional suffix, `opencode`, `codex`, `cursor`) and specify whether both bare and versioned `claude-code` forms are simultaneously valid, so all three vocabulary-alignment edits are driven by a single authoritative list.
findings.finding: The plan introduces bare `claude-code` as a new valid `AI_AGENT` / `claim_harness` vocabulary value, but the complete updated canonical vocabulary for the field is never defined anywhere — the schema (`ticket_state.schema.json`) types `claim_harness` as free-form `string | null` with no `enum`, so the vocabulary is entirely informal/doc-driven; the existing doc examples (`claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` / `codex` / `cursor`) are stated in `session_id.py` line 45, `docs/config.md` line 352, and `docs/event-schema.md` line 145, but the plan never specifies whether `claude-code_<ver>` (versioned) remains valid alongside bare `claude-code`, nor does it enumerate what all valid base names are after the change.
findings.location: What section — Vocabulary alignment; AC6; src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py line 45; docs/config.md line 352; docs/event-schema.md line 145
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table or bullet list in the What section (Vocabulary alignment) explicitly enumerating all valid `AI_AGENT` values after the change, e.g.: base names `claude-code`, `opencode`, `codex`, `cursor`; optional `_<version>` suffix on any base name. State that `claude-code_<ver>` remains valid (additive, not deprecated). Then update `session_id.py` line 45 comment to reflect this list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rationale in the plan (e.g. in `docs/session-id-shims.md` or the Acceptance Criteria) for why a live Claude Code integration test is omitted from CI, and optionally add a manual smoke-test step (e.g. 'fire the hook manually, inspect `$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`') as a non-CI acceptance note.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a hook script whose core live-execution path — Claude Code actually firing the SessionStart hook and writing to `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` — has no acceptance criterion that exercises it end-to-end against a real Claude Code session. All ACs use mocked/offline pytest assertions. E6(f) requires either an end-to-end exercise of the live path or an explicitly stated rationale for its absence (e.g. 'live integration deferred to manual smoke-test in install docs because CI cannot launch Claude Code'). The plan is silent on this.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs) and ## What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a non-CI AC such as: 'Manual smoke-test (not automated): after installing the hook per `docs/session-id-shims.md`, start a Claude Code session and confirm `REBAR_SESSION_ID` and `AI_AGENT` are set in `$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` (rationale: CI cannot launch Claude Code; the hook runtime boundary is validated manually at install time).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` (modification to add the docs-vocabulary assertions) to the Scope IN list, or replace the AC #6 proof command with one using `test_session_shims.py` to keep all new test logic in one file.
findings.finding: AC #6's proof command references `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` with new assertions (old slash-form absent, new base-name form present), but the Scope names only `tests/unit/test_session_shims.py` as the test artifact in-scope — leaving modifications to `test_harness_provenance.py` as undeclared work.
findings.location: Scope section (test file) vs. AC #6 proof step
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope IN: 'extending `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` with doc-vocabulary assertions for the base-name vocabulary change.' Alternatively, move the AC #6 doc-content assertions into a new test function in `test_session_shims.py` and update the proof command accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (and test) that the strip step uses a hook-specific sentinel/prefix comment or a uniquely-owned variable name to avoid clobbering AI_AGENT lines written by sibling shims, OR explicitly acknowledge and justify that this hook owns and overwrites AI_AGENT unconditionally.
findings.finding: The idempotent re-fire logic strips ALL lines matching `REBAR_SESSION_ID` and `AI_AGENT` variable names from the env file, not just lines written by this specific hook. If a sibling hook (e.g., a Cursor or Codex shim) has already written `export AI_AGENT=cursor` to the same env file, the Claude Code shim's re-fire will silently delete that line, clobbering a cooperating hook's output without acknowledgement.
findings.location: What section — idempotency description: 'first STRIPS this hook's own prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports from the env file (preserving other hooks' lines) then re-writes'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) have the script emit a sentinel comment (e.g., `# rebar-claude-code-shim`) before its two export lines and strip only lines in that block on re-fire, or (b) explicitly state in the plan that the Claude Code shim is authoritative for AI_AGENT in a Claude Code session and that sibling shims must not co-fire, and add an AC test confirming a pre-existing `export AI_AGENT=other` line is intentionally overwritten (documenting the design decision, not just the outcome).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step to link `docs/session-id-shims.md` from at least one navigable entry point (e.g., the top-level README's 'Session Provenance' section or a `docs/index.md` / `docs/README.md` table of contents).
findings.finding: The new `docs/session-id-shims.md` page is not linked from any existing docs index, README, or navigation entry, making it undiscoverable for users who haven't read the full spec.
findings.location: Docs section — 'Docs (docs/session-id-shims.md)' and 'Vocabulary alignment' entries; no mention of updating README or docs/index.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the hook→env-file injection architectural decision (why CLAUDE_ENV_FILE env-file injection vs. direct env export or rc-file alternatives, graceful-degradation contract) in an ADR or a `CLAUDE.md` / design-doc section, not just an install guide.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — a hook-injected env-file (`CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`) as the shared channel for session provenance — but does not specify capturing this decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update. The `docs/session-id-shims.md` page is an install guide, not an architectural decision record.
findings.location: ## What – Hook script description; ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/session-hook-env-injection.md`) or a 'Design Notes' section in `docs/session-id-shims.md` covering: the rationale for CLAUDE_ENV_FILE as the injection channel, the considered alternatives, and the graceful-degradation contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least a `stderr` warning (or a `--debug`/`REBAR_SHIM_DEBUG` flag) for non-trivial silent no-op cases (e.g., CLAUDE_ENV_FILE set but unwritable, session_id field absent from parsed JSON) so users and operators can distinguish misconfiguration from normal no-op.
findings.finding: All failure modes (malformed JSON, missing CLAUDE_ENV_FILE, blank session_id) are silent no-ops with no observability instrumentation. There is no log line, metric, or trace that allows an operator or user to distinguish 'shim fired correctly' from 'shim silently failed to populate REBAR_SESSION_ID due to misconfiguration'.
findings.location: ## What – 'Uses python3... any parse failure... is a silent no-op with exit 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Emit a one-line stderr message (e.g., `echo '[rebar/session-shim] WARN: session_id missing from hook payload' >&2`) for cases where CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is set but session_id is absent or unwritable — these indicate misconfiguration, unlike the fully-absent CLAUDE_ENV_FILE case which is a legitimate no-op.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the strip-then-rewrite of CLAUDE_ENV_FILE uses an atomic file operation (e.g., `flock` for exclusive locking, or write-to-tempfile + `mv` atomic rename) to prevent TOCTOU races under concurrent hook invocations.
findings.finding: The strip-then-rewrite pattern on CLAUDE_ENV_FILE (read file, strip matching lines, write back) is a non-atomic read-modify-write (TOCTOU race). If two hook invocations fire concurrently (e.g., subagent spawn + parent session resume), both could read the file before either writes, causing one update to be silently lost or the file to contain duplicate/interleaved lines.
findings.location: ## What – 'Idempotent: re-firing on resume/clear/compact first STRIPS this hook's own prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports from the env file (preserving other hooks' lines) then re-writes the current values'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `flock` in the Python script: `import fcntl; fcntl.flock(f, fcntl.LOCK_EX)` before reading and releasing after writing, or write to a tempfile and `os.replace()` (atomic rename) to make the update atomic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'verify installation' step to `docs/session-id-shims.md` (e.g., a one-liner users can run inside a Claude Code session to confirm `REBAR_SESSION_ID` was set), and note the AC should assert this section exists.
findings.finding: The install UX documented in `docs/session-id-shims.md` has no described 'verify the hook is active' step, leaving users with no in-band way to confirm the shim is firing correctly after install.
findings.location: What section — 'Docs (`docs/session-id-shims.md`)'; AC for docs content
findings.suggested_fix: In the docs AC, add a check for a verification snippet such as 'After the first SessionStart, run `echo $REBAR_SESSION_ID` inside the session — a non-empty value confirms the shim is active.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete citation (e.g. a pinned Claude Code changelog entry, docs permalink with version anchor, or a quoted snippet from the hooks reference) that confirms `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the exact variable name and append-file semantics are as described, so the executor can verify the external-API assumption before shipping.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is 'the Claude Code hook-contract' env-injection variable (with append-via-`>>` semantics), citing `https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks` — but `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` appears zero times anywhere in the codebase (src, docs, tests, scripts). The external URL cannot be verified by tools, making this an unverifiable external-API claim that the entire shim's correctness hinges on.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description / CLAUDE_ENV_FILE mechanism

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the two unverified Claude Code CLI capability assertions before implementation: (a) confirm `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct and current env-injection variable name by citing a verifiable, version-tagged Claude Code docs URL (e.g. on docs.anthropic.com), and (b) confirm the SessionStart hook stdin envelope uses a `session_id` (snake_case) field — add both the confirmed URL and a representative fixture of the actual JSON envelope to the plan or script header.
findings.finding: The plan's entire injection mechanism rests on two unverified Claude Code CLI capability assertions: (1) that `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env var name for the hook-to-session env-injection file, and (2) that the SessionStart hook delivers a `session_id` field on stdin. Neither claim appears anywhere in the repo (zero hits for `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` outside plan-review cache), and the cited reference URL (`https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks`) is non-canonical and cannot be confirmed. If either assertion is wrong, the shim silently no-ops for ALL users with no diagnostic path.
findings.location: What section (Hook script description) — 'the SessionStart stdin `session_id` field and the `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` env-injection file… per the Claude Code hooks reference (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the unverifiable URL `https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks` with the canonical Anthropic docs URL (e.g. `https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks`), add a pinned version tag or date-of-verification note, and include a minimal representative SessionStart JSON envelope excerpt (e.g. `{"type":"SessionStart","session_id":"…"}`) in the script header comments so the assumed field name is explicitly documented and reviewable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct Claude Code hook env-injection variable name (e.g., by cross-referencing a pinned version of the hooks reference, a local `claude --help`, or a known-good `.claude/settings.json` snippet) and add a repo-checkable citation (version tag or snapshot URL) so reviewers can confirm the external-contract fact independently.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` — the env var name the entire injection mechanism depends on — is asserted as 'the documented Claude Code env-injection file' per the cited URL (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks), but this specific name appears zero times in the codebase and the URL cannot be verified from the repo environment. If the actual Claude Code CLI uses a different var name (e.g., `CLAUDE_HOOK_ENV`), the shim silently no-ops for every user, defeating the story's purpose.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description; cited URL https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the script header and docs page a pinned-version citation (e.g., 'Claude Code v1.x, hooks reference § Environment, verified YYYY-MM-DD') and, if possible, add a one-time local smoke-test to the acceptance criteria: run the hook script inside an actual Claude Code session and confirm `REBAR_SESSION_ID` is set, to close the unverified-external-capability gap before shipping.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact JSON field name (`session_id` vs `sessionId` vs other) in the Claude Code SessionStart hook stdin envelope via a verifiable source (pinned docs version, running `claude` locally with a probe hook, or a known-good community example) and add that confirmation as a citation in the script header.
findings.finding: The `session_id` field name in the SessionStart stdin JSON envelope is asserted as the correct hook-contract field but is unverified from the repo — it does not appear in any hook-related file and the cited URL cannot be confirmed. If the actual field is named differently (e.g., `sessionId`, `id`), the shim always silently no-ops (python3 parses OK but finds no `session_id` → blank → exit 0).
findings.location: What section — Hook script description; AC2 (`{"session_id":"abc123"}`)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for CLAUDE_ENV_FILE entries: either specify a SessionEnd/PostToolUse hook that removes the REBAR_SESSION_ID and AI_AGENT lines, or document in `docs/session-id-shims.md` that CLAUDE_ENV_FILE MUST be a session-scoped tempfile (and show how to create one) that is automatically deleted when the session ends.
findings.finding: There is no RETIRE/cleanup path specified for the CLAUDE_ENV_FILE entries written by the shim. If CLAUDE_ENV_FILE points to a persistent file (e.g., sourced from a shell profile or a long-lived dotfile), stale REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports will persist across shell sessions after the Claude Code session ends.
findings.location: ## What – Hook script description; docs/session-id-shims.md caveat G3
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/session-id-shims.md, show a settings.json example where CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is set to a mktemp-generated path in a shell wrapper (e.g., `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE=$(mktemp) claude ...`) so the tempfile is session-scoped and cleaned up by the OS, eliminating the need for an explicit retire hook.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (in plan text or a new AC) that S5's `claim_harness` parsing accepts the bare `claude-code` value without a version suffix, or add a compatibility note/fix if it currently requires the versioned form.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a bare `claude-code` AI_AGENT value and updates vocabulary examples away from `claude-code_<ver>`, but does not confirm that existing S5 consumers (claim_harness downstream logic) tolerate the bare form without a version suffix.
findings.location: What section — 'The harness tag is the base name `claude-code`'; Vocabulary alignment AC; Depends on: S5
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or inline note: 'S5 consumers accept bare `claude-code` (verified by `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k bare_base_name`)' — or document that S5 already treats `_<version>` as optional in its matching logic.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
